signals.raw: Based on the search results provided, the most recent inflation-related developments are from April 10, 2026 (over a week ago by your current date):
matched_markets: Will annual inflation increase by 3.8% in April?

signals.headline: Market Impact:**
signals.raw:
Market Impact:**
This development significantly constrains Federal Reserve rate cut expectations. **Core inflation remained elevated at 2.6% year-over-year** in March, suggesting price pressures extend beyond volatile energy costs.[2][5] Goldman Sachs revised its December 2026 headline PCE inflation forecast upward by a full percentage point due to the war, with most of that revision attributable

signals.headline: Fed Policy Implications:**
signals.raw:
Fed Policy Implications:**
Fed policymakers are now cautious about additional interest rate cuts, at least until they can assess how long-lasting these wartime price increases will be.[5] However, Goldman expects core goods inflation to decelerate sharply as tariff passthrough cycles run their course, potentially enabling rate cuts later in 2026.[1]
